description: This figure consists of two subplots characterizing grain size evolution in Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.8C low-density steel subjected to 250℃ warm multiaxial forging (MAF) with 0, 1, 3, 5 passes (denoted MAF-0, MAF-1, MAF-3, MAF-5). Subplot (a) plots area fraction against grain size: as MAF pass count increases, the grain size distribution shifts to smaller values and becomes progressively narrower and more uniform, with peak area fraction decreasing for larger grains and increasing for finer grains, confirming progressive grain refinement during deformation. Subplot (b) shows the relationship between equivalent plastic strain and average grain size, with measured data points fitted by an exponential model (d = 50*exp(-0.63999*x)) that achieves an R-square of 0.99052, indicating excellent prediction accuracy. Average grain size reduces from 50 μm at 0 strain to ~13 μm at 2.3 equivalent strain (5 MAF passes). This figure validates the strain-dependent grain refinement law applied in the modified Johnson-Cook constitutive model for predicting the steel’s plastic flow behavior during MAF.
